channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canberra man faces court over alleged assault on security guards
item.description: Grant William Denahy, 38, appeared in the ACT Magistrates Court on Monday, May 11, accused of assaulting two security guards at the Canberra Centre and fleeing the scene. The alleged incident occurred...

item.title: Understanding and Addressing Racial and Ethnic Health Inequalities in Canada
item.description: Health disparities among racial and ethnic groups persist in Canada, yet effectively addressing these inequalities requires a distinct Canadian approach. Much of current research is adapted from U.S. ...

item.title: Fhatuwani Mukheli on Dyslexia, Artistic Journey, and Celebrating Black Lives
item.description: Fhatuwani Mukheli, the 41-year-old multi-disciplinary artist, shares his journey of overcoming dyslexia and celebrating Black lives through his art. Born in Soweto and spending festive periods in Vend...

item.title: India and Vietnam Elevate Bilateral Relations Amid Indo-Pacific Changes
item.description: The state visit of Vietnamese President Tô Lâm to India from May 5-7, 2026, represents a significant milestone in India-Vietnam relations. Both countries have agreed to elevate their ties to an Enhanc...

item.title: EACOP Launches Welding Training Programme to Strengthen Tanzanian Skills
item.description: Dodoma — The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) imeanza initiative mpya ya kuimarisha local content kwa kuwapa fursa Watanzania kushiriki kikamilifu kwenye miradi mikubwa ya kitaifa, hasa kwenye ...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte expresses gratitude to supporters amid impeachment proceedings
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has expressed her sincere gratitude to her supporters while in The Hague, where she is visiting her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte. This comes amid the House of R...

item.title: Government Postpones Tax Relief for Low-Income Workers Amid Budget Concerns
item.description: The Kenyan government has decided to delay the implementation of income tax relief aimed at low-income earners, citing concerns over a projected Sh35 billion revenue shortfall. This proposal, initiall...

item.title: IFC and Standard Chartered partner to expand supply chain finance access for African SMEs
item.description: International Finance Corporation (IFC) and Standard Chartered don team up to launch one $300 million risk-sharing facility wey go help improve access to supply chain finance for businesses across Afr...

item.title: Ramaphosa affirms he will remain President despite Constitutional Court ruling
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced that he will not resign, following a Constitutional Court ruling concerning the Phala Phala matter and the commencement of parliamentary steps towards an impeac...

item.title: Lafarge Africa Empowers 100 Persons with Disabilities Through Skill Development Programme
item.description: For Nigeria, plenty people wey get disabilities dey face wahala every day for school, work and financial independence. Lafarge Africa no just dey look the problem, dem dey solve am actively. Under the...

item.title: The Making and Impact of the Controversial Final Scene in John Waters’ 'Pink Flamingos'
item.description: The 1972 cult film 'Pink Flamingos,' directed by John Waters, is notorious for its final scene in which the drag performer Divine eats dog feces, an act designed to shock audiences and solidify her ch...

item.title: Trump Refuses Iran's Proposal, Emphasises Full US Victory in Middle East Conflict
item.description: On May 11, 2026, President Donald Trump firmly rejected Iran's recent counteroffer aimed at ending the ongoing Middle East war. Tehran had proposed a deal that included the release of frozen Iranian a...

item.title: Retired citizen dies preventing suicide attack near Kohat checkpoint
item.description: In a brave act of selflessness, a retired Pakistan Railways employee named Liaqat laid down his life while stopping a suicide bomber near Kohat district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. The incident took place ...

item.title: ICPC moves to arraign FCTA official Anita Odom for alleged certificate forgery
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) don apply to FCT High Court, Nyanya, to get court date to arraign Mrs Anita Anurika Odom, one senior staff for Federal Ca...

item.title: Centre to implement VB–G RAM G rural employment scheme from July 1, replacing MGNREGA
item.description: The Union Government has officially notified that the Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act, 2025, will be implemented from 1st July 2026. This new framework, known as...

item.title: France Passes Law to Simplify Return of Colonial-Era African Artworks
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron don dey hail di new law wey France parliament pass to make am easy for dem to return African artworks wey dem loot during colonial days. E be like say na big step forw...

item.title: Political Connections Often Decide Zanzibar Youth Employment, Not Qualifications
item.description: For many young people in Zanzibar, especially in villages like Mahonda, having a university degree or technical diploma is no guarantee of getting a job. Youth who joined a recent dialogue under the '...

item.title: Scientists Conduct Controlled Seismic Experiments Beneath the Swiss Alps
item.description: Reports suggest that Swiss researchers have carried out controlled seismic experiments beneath the Swiss Alps. These operations involved deliberately triggering small-scale earthquakes to study the ge...

item.title: Omar Abdullah and Amit Shah discuss restoration of J&K statehood and governance issues
item.description: On Monday, Union Home Minister Amit Shah met with Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah in New Delhi to discuss multiple issues concerning the region. The central focus of their discussion wa...

item.title: Carney proposes regulatory reforms to speed up major projects, shifting focus from strict environmental activism
item.description: Canada’s new government, under Prime Minister Carney, is signalling a shift in approach to environmental and industrial policy. Despite Carney’s campaign pledge to accelerate construction and developm...
